message:
perf(bd-wwqen.3): leaf-local integer CountIndexEqRun fast path, batched memdb publish, and prepared UPDATE/DELETE DML direct handoff

This commit advances the bd-wwqen benchmark recovery epic (comprehensive
benchmark catastrophe vs. legacy C SQLite) across five interconnected
subsystems. The changes address three major performance bottlenecks
surfaced by the 2026-03-25 benchmark rerun: (1) CountIndexEqRun
per-row decode overhead on sorted integer index scans, (2) retained
:memory: autocommit publish-path O(n) page sweeps and redundant
cloning, and (3) repeated prepared UPDATE/DELETE statements inside
explicit transactions paying per-write MemDatabase reload costs.

## B-tree cursor: leaf-local integer first-key probing (fsqlite-btree)

The CountIndexEqRun VDBE opcode counts consecutive index entries whose
first key column matches a probe value. Previously this required a
full payload decode per row. For the overwhelmingly common case where
the first column is an integer stored in the leaf cell's local payload
(no overflow), the new `first_index_key_integer_from_local_payload`
family of methods extracts and compares the value directly from the
on-page bytes without ever materializing a SqliteValue or touching the
decode cache. A new `count_equal_first_index_key_run_integer_local_segment`
method batch-scans an entire leaf page's worth of cells in a tight loop,
only falling back to the generic path when it encounters non-integer
serial types or overflow pages.

Two additional correctness fixes in the B-tree cursor are included:

- `count_all_rows` now includes interior separator cells in index
  COUNT, matching the cursor traversal semantics where interior index
  entries are logical rows. Without this, COUNT(*) on large indexes
  undercounted by the number of interior separator entries.

- Upward split propagation during insert now recomputes the child slot
  via `find_child_slot_by_page_no` instead of trusting the stale
  cursor-stack `cell_idx`. After a lower-level balance rewrites the
  tree shape, the stale index could point at the wrong child, causing
  silent data loss in deep monotonic insert workloads (surfaced by the
  10k unique-email insert test).

- `index_insert_unique` no longer attempts to reuse the post-probe
  cursor position for the subsequent insert. The successor/EOF state
  after the uniqueness check was unreliable for deep monotonic
  unique-key streams, causing undercounts on the exact 10k
  unique-email workload.

## VDBE engine: integer-specialized CountIndexEqRun dispatch (fsqlite-vdbe)

The engine's CountIndexEqRun handler now dispatches integer probe
values through a dedicated fast path that:

1. Attempts a zero-copy leaf-local comparison via the new btree cursor
   API, avoiding payload buffer allocation entirely.
2. Falls back to a prefix-buffer probe that parses only the record
   header and first column's serial type, caching the decoded integer
   on mismatch so the next opcode can reuse it without re-reading.
3. Only reaches the generic `decode_column_from_offset_reuse` path
   for non-integer serial types (floats, text, blobs).

The old `ensure_storage_cursor_row_cache` function that eagerly loaded
the full payload on every position change is removed; the
CountIndexEqRun hot path now manages position stamps and payload
buffers directly, avoiding redundant work.

The storage-cursor COUNT opcode no longer shortcuts through the
connection-local MemDatabase mirror. The mirror can lag retained
same-connection writes (the exact scenario that caused the benchmark
catastrophe), so COUNT now always walks the cursor's own B-tree.

The codegen layer now distinguishes IN-subquery probe sources that
qualify for direct (non-materialized) probing from those that require
once-materialized fallback, enabling the engine to skip ephemeral
table construction for bounded IPK-projected subqueries.

## Pager: conditional page sweep and drain-based publish (fsqlite-pager)

The `publish_commit` path previously swept all published pages on every
commit via `retain(|page_no| page_no <= db_size)`. For retained
autocommit workloads that never shrink the database, this was pure
O(n) overhead inside the commit roundtrip. The sweep now only runs
when the publication actually shrinks the visible database size AND
the publication is not stale relative to the current published commit
horizon (preventing an older smaller publication from evicting pages
from a newer larger snapshot).

The prebuilt-page publish path is replaced by a consuming
`publish_commit_draining_write_set` that moves staged pages directly
into the published state via `into_published_page()`, avoiding the
clone-then-clear pattern. The write set is drained in place so the
HashMap allocation is preserved for reuse without an explicit
`clear()` call.

## VFS: batched page writes for MemoryFile (fsqlite-vfs)

A new `write_page_batch` trait method on `VfsFile` allows the pager to
flush multiple dirty pages in a single logical operation. The default
implementation issues sequential `write` calls, but `MemoryFile`
overrides it to acquire the backing-storage mutex once for the entire
batch, amortizing lock overhead for the retained :memory: commit path
that previously locked/unlocked per page.

## Connection: prepared UPDATE/DELETE DML direct handoff (fsqlite-core)

Prepared UPDATE/DELETE statements with a fast-path classification
(no triggers, no WITHOUT ROWID, no live virtual tables, no foreign key
cascade) now precompute and cache their `TableExecutionRuntimeInputs`
at prepare time. When executed inside an explicit transaction, these
statements take a new "DML direct handoff" path that:

1. Skips the pre-write MemDatabase reload when the mirror is already
   marked stale from a prior write in the same transaction, avoiding
   O(n) row re-materialization between consecutive writes.
2. Hands the active transaction directly to the VDBE engine without
   re-acquiring it, eliminating redundant autocommit setup.
3. Defers MemDatabase staleness tracking to the next read boundary
   instead of paying per-write mirror upkeep.

The `reload_memdb_rows_from_txn_preserving_schema` path during
retained autocommit finalization is simplified: the schema cookie and
generation are no longer round-tripped through a preserved identity
tuple, and the `memdb_requires_active_txn_reload` flag is now set
unconditionally when count shortcuts become unsafe (previously gated
on `!had_active_txn`, which missed the retained-txn case).

The prepared fast-path eligibility check for statement savepoint
skipping is relaxed: columns with NOT NULL constraints no longer
disqualify a prepared UPDATE from the fast path, since the constraint
check happens inside the VDBE engine regardless.

The `reload_memdb_from_active_txn_snapshot` helper now preserves the
database header's change counter through the reload, preventing stale
change-counter comparisons after a retained autocommit snapshot refresh.

## MVCC: HTM guard dead_code annotations (fsqlite-mvcc)

Phase 1 HTM (Hardware Transactional Memory) guard scaffolding constants
and methods that are defined but not yet wired into the flat combiner
acquire path receive `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations with a comment
explaining they are Phase 1 scaffolding to be wired in later phases.
This silences compiler warnings without removing the intentional
forward declarations.

## Beads tracker update

The issues.jsonl tracker is updated with campaign progress notes for
the bd-wwqen benchmark recovery epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
